name: IBM
summary: IBM (International Business Machines) has been involved in artificial intelligence since the field's inception. Nathaniel Rochester, an IBM engineer, co-organized the 1956 Dartmouth Conference that founded AI as a discipline. IBM created Deep Blue, the chess-playing computer that defeated world champion Garry Kasparov in 1997, and Watson, the question-answering system that won Jeopardy! in 2011. While IBM's influence in AI has waned relative to newer entrants like Google and OpenAI, its historical contributions — including pioneering mainframe computing, early neural network simulations, and high-profile AI demonstrations — cemented its place in the field's lineage.
